title: On (243,121,60)-Difference Sets in C3 x C9 x C9
lean_gate_reason: The strongest surviving claim is only a supporting quotient-profile proposition. Lean would formalize a structural side lemma, not the decisive obstruction-or-witness needed for a human-ready packet on the selected problem.
micro_paper_assessment: The selected problem remains a strong micro-paper target if the exact instance closes, but the audited output is only a structural quotient slice. It is stronger than a toy example, yet still too incomplete to count as a micro-paper packet or a near-paper theorem slice.
strongest_honest_claim: If a (243,121,60)-difference set exists in C3 x C9 x C9, then after 61-multiplier normalization its image in G/G[3] ~= C3^2 has one coset of weight 13, three opposite coset pairs of weights {12,15}, and one opposite coset pair of weights {9,18}.
candidate_theorem_slice: If a (243,121,60)-difference set exists in C3 x C9 x C9, then after 61-multiplier normalization its image in G/G[3] ~= C3^2 has one coset of weight 13, three opposite coset pairs of weights {12,15}, and one opposite coset pair of weights {9,18}.
paper_title_hint: A Multiplier-Normalized Quotient Constraint for Putative (243,121,60)-Difference Sets in C3 x C9 x C9
